mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate that remains above its trailing 12-month average signals a persistently tight monetary stance, raising the discount rate applied to equity cash flows and compressing valuation multiples, producing a persistent drag on S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and forward 20d returns stays below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or high-rate periods show statistically indistinguishable returns from low-rate periods (p>0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month window, it signals an inflection toward labor market deterioration that historically precedes earnings downgrades, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 20d returns following a >0.3pp 3-month UNRATE rise are positive in more than 55% of triggered historical instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous tightening in FEDFUNDS and deceleration in PAYEMS growth jointly indicate a policy-induced slowdown that is not captured by either series alone; this combined signal predicts negative S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon as markets price a higher probability of recession.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS above 3-month rolling average AND PAYEMS 3-month annualized growth below 0.5%
regime_hint: monetary tightening combined with labor deceleration
failure_condition: Joint-trigger instances show 60d forward returns statistically indistinguishable from unconditional baseline (p>0.10), or interaction term adds no incremental Rank IC over either single-series model.

mechanism_narrative: Declining housing starts (HOUST) during a period of elevated federal funds rate reflect suppressed rate-sensitive demand that leads broader economic activity; in a monetary tightening regime this combination predicts negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days, but is uninformative in easing regimes.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 4.0% AND HOUST declines > 5% over trailing 6 months
failure_condition: During periods meeting the trigger, 60d forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances, or the effect reverses sign when tested in an easing-regime control sample.

mechanism_narrative: A rising trend in INDPRO reflects expanding industrial output and corporate top-line growth, which is incorporated relatively quickly into equity prices, producing modest positive S&P 500 returns over the following 5 trading days as short-horizon momentum in real activity data propagates to risk assets.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no significant positive skew conditional on INDPRO month-over-month increases (t-test p>0.05) across a 5-year rolling sample.

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales (RSAFS) growth accelerates above its trailing 6-month average, it signals robust consumer demand that supports corporate revenue expectations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month growth exceeds trailing 6-month average by > 0.5pp
failure_condition: Triggered instances of accelerating RSAFS growth yield forward 20d returns not significantly different from baseline (p>0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint pattern of rising PAYEMS alongside falling RSAFS growth indicates labor market strength decoupling from consumer spending, a divergence historically associated with margin compression and negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon as earnings disappoint despite low unemployment.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month growth positive AND RSAFS 3-month growth negative
regime_hint: late-cycle divergence between labor and consumption
failure_condition: Divergence-trigger instances produce forward 60d returns statistically indistinguishable from baseline (p>0.10), or the sign of the effect is inconsistent across at least two distinct historical divergence episodes.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden acceleration in CPIAUCSL month-over-month change above 0.4% signals an inflation surprise that raises expectations of future rate hikes, causing an immediate risk-off repricing and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following a >0.4% MoM CPI surprise are positive in more than 55% of historical instances, or the average abnormal return is not statistically distinguishable from zero (p>0.05).
